records.ety: [OE. purchasen, porchacen, OF. porchacier, purchacier, to pursue, to seek eagerly, F. pourchasser; OF. pour, por, pur, for (L. pro) + chacier to pursue, to chase. See Chase.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Purchased (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Purchasing.]

defs: To pursue and obtain; to acquire by seeking; to gain, obtain, or acquire.
quotes: That loves the thing he can not purchase.
Your accent is Something finer than you could purchase in so removed a dwelling.
His faults . . . hereditary Rather than purchased.

defs: To obtain by paying money or its equivalent; to buy for a price; as, to purchase land, or a house.
quotes: The field which Abraham purchased of the sons of Heth.

defs: To obtain by any outlay, as of labor, danger, or sacrifice, etc.; as, to purchase favor with flattery.
quotes: One poor retiring minute . . . Would purchase thee a thousand thousand friends.
A world who would not purchase with a bruise?

defs: To expiate by a fine or forfeit.
quotes: Not tears nor prayers shall purchase out abuses.

defs: To acquire by any means except descent or inheritance.
To apply to (anything) a device for obtaining a mechanical advantage; to get a purchase upon, or apply a purchase to; as, to purchase a cannon.
To put forth effort to obtain anything; to strive; to exert one's self.
quotes: Duke John of Brabant purchased greatly that the Earl of Flanders should have his daughter in marriage.
Sure our lawyers Would not purchase half so fast.

records.ety: [OE. purchds, F. pourchas eager pursuit. See Purchase, v. t.]

defs: The act of seeking, getting, or obtaining anything.
quotes: I'll . . . get meat to have thee, Or lose my life in the purchase.

defs: The act of seeking and acquiring property.
The acquisition of title to, or properly in, anything for a price; buying for money or its equivalent.
quotes: It is foolish to lay out money in the purchase of repentance.

defs: That which is obtained, got, or acquired, in any manner, honestly or dishonestly; property; possession; acquisition.
quotes: We met with little purchase upon this coast, except two small vessels of Golconda.
A beauty-waning and distressed widow . . . Made prize and purchase of his lustful eye.

defs: That which is obtained for a price in money or its equivalent.

Any mechanical hold, or advantage, applied to the raising or removing of heavy bodies, as by a lever, a tackle, capstan, and the like; also, the apparatus, tackle, or device by which the advantage is gained.
quotes: A politician, to do great things, looks for a power -- what our workmen call a purchase.

defs: Acquisition of lands or tenements by other means than descent or inheritance, namely, by one's own act or agreement.
